heading: Tonight
text: Rain from the southern Adirondacks and Rt. 2 south, a chance of showers north, tapering off west to east before midnight, likely to dry out over Vermont before morning. Lows ranging through the 50s. Winds becoming light and variable.

heading: Tuesday
text: Morning clouds, any showers east, mostly in NH ending. Increasing periods of sunshine northwest to southeast. A slight chance of an afternoon shower north. Highs in the upper 70s in the valleys, low 70s in the northern mountains. Breezy west northwest winds gusting up to 15.

heading: Tuesday Night
text: Becoming mostly clear, except periods of clouds north, and a stray evening shower. Patchy valley fog. Lows in the 50s, near 60 around Burlington. Winds northwest near 10 mph, diminishing to light and variable.
